 About us 
 SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering (SITE) is not just one more engineering college. It's a college with a difference.

 Years of Service in Education Sector 
 SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering 
 SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering started its functioning with four academic programs like CSE, ECE, EEE & ME. Now the Institute offers Ten UG programs (CE, CSE,  ECE, EEE, ME, IT, CSM, CSD, CIC & AIML); Five PG programs (CSE, PE, VLSI &ES, CAD/CAM, & MBA), Five Research programs (ECE, CSE, EEE, ME & Physics) and Four Diploma programs (ECE, EEE, ME & CME). It created a sensation in the field of engineering education by receiving NAAC with A+ Grade, NBA accreditations, UGC 2(f) & 12(B) status, DSIR SIRO, AICTE IDEA Lab and permanent affiliation from JNTUK.

 SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering 
 Vision & Mission 
 Institute Vision 
 Aspire to be a leading institute in professional education by creating technocrats to propel societal transformations through inventions and innovations. 
 Institute Mission 
 •⁠ ⁠To impart technology integrated active learning environment that nurtures the technical & life skills. •⁠ ⁠To enhance scientific temper through active research leading to innovations & sustainable environment •⁠ ⁠To create responsible citizens with highest ethical standards. 
 Quality Policy 
 To achieve excellence in education, research and consultancy by creating conducive environment that facilitate continuous improvement, with professionalism at its core and global outlook. 
 What Our Students Say About Us 
 Hear our students share how our guidance, teaching methods, and support transformed their learning journey. 
 GOOD Based on 1081 reviews Posted on Google rambabu ghantasala Trustindex verifies that the original source of the review is Google. SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering is a good college, especially for training and cultural activities. The college focuses well on student development and provides regular placement training, soft skills classes, and technical workshops that help students prepare for their careers. The training programs are useful and improve confidence and communication skills.
The college also encourages students to participate in cultural events, fests, and extracurricular activities. These activities help students showcase their talents and enjoy campus life along with academics. Faculty members are supportive and guide students in both academics and activities.
Overall, SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering is a good choice for students who want balanced growth, with proper training opportunities and an active cultural environment. Posted on Google kranthi karri Trustindex verifies that the original source of the review is Google. The campus is clean, organized, and disciplined, with decent infrastructure such as classrooms, labs, library, and hostel facilities. SIT gives importance to placements and training, offering aptitude classes, technical training, and soft-skill development to help students prepare for campus recruitment.

 Frequently Asked 
 Questions. 
 Is SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering an autonomous institution? 
 Yes. SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering (SITE) is an autonomous engineering institution. 
 Which university is the institute affiliated to?  
 The institute is permanently affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Kakinada (JNTUK). 
 Is the institute approved by AICTE?  
 Yes. SITE is approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). 
 What accreditations does the institute have? 
 The institute is accredited with NAAC A+ Grade and has NBA accreditation for select programs. 
 Where is the institute located?  
 SITE is located in Tadepalligudem, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh. 
 What programs does the institute offer?  
 The institute offers undergraduate, postgraduate, research, and diploma programs in engineering and management disciplines. 
 How are admissions conducted? 
 Admissions are conducted as per the norms and guidelines of APSCHE and the affiliating university through state-level counseling processes. 
 Does the institute offer postgraduate programs?  
 Yes. SITE offers postgraduate programs in engineering and management, including MBA. 
 Does the institute have research programs?  
 Yes. The institute offers research programs in select engineering and science disciplines. 
 How can parents or students contact the institute?  
 Contact details including address, phone numbers, and official email ID are available on the Contact page of the website. 
 Our MoU's with over 200+ companies 
 Join more than 200 companies that have already hired our students.

title: Sri B. Venu Gopala Krishna - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Sri B. Venu Gopala Krishna - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Chairman 
 Sri Burugupalli Venu Gopala Krishna 
 “I am greatly honored to serve the society as President of SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering at Tadepalligudem. At SITE, students are trained to become not only efficient Engineers but also good people who render great service to the humanity in all aspects. As production, software and service industries are shifting to India, our country needs lakhs of Engineers to fulfil the demand. These Engineers need to be creative in thinking, innovative in execution, proficient in oral and written communication, able to work for longer hours effectively in teams, on multi – disciplinary projects. In fact, these are our core teaching values at our SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering.” 
 Chairman Profile 
 Shri Burugupalli Venu Gopala Krishna, the President, SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering is a well known personality in the field of education for the last 40+ years in coastal districts of Andhra Pradesh. He believes in hard work and always says Success is measured not by what you create for yourself but by what you leave behind. As a man of integrity and honesty, he sets an example for all and loves to stay with the students in the campus, motivating and moulding them into ideal students.In the highly competitive field of education, it may be a glorious dream for many an educationist to see his school as the best and get an award at least once in life. But Mr. B. Venu Gopala Krishna, has outsmarted everyone by winning the state best school award four times consecutively. It is testimony for his life long devotion for the cause of education. Mr. B. Venu Gopala Krishna, the son of a small farmer, is now a lord of an educational empire which has more than 15, 000 students. One can understand well, the meaning of commitment and dedication when one walks through the corridors of the schools and the colleges. By starting his school in the small village of Velivennu, he has proved it loud and clear that wherever you work with commitment and dedication, you will make a mark and attract the attention of millions. The schools he started get the best school awards consecutively, the junior colleges he established produce many national and state level ranks year after year, but his thirst for service in the field of education still remains unquenchable and insatiable. It is no exaggeration to say that he stands as a role model for many young enthusiastic educationists. With his leadership, SASI English Medium School has bagged state best school award five times in a row!. He recieved TVN – KIDAO – Outstanding Education Institution- 2014 award for SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering from National Institution for Quality and Reliability, Chennai. He is actively involved in social service and generous in donating a lot to CMs Relief Fund, Cargil Relief Fund, Helpage India and other social service organizations. He is keen in rural development and thus in the process he established most of the educational institutions in rural areas.

title: Prof. Mohammed Ismail - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Prof. Mohammed Ismail - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Principal 
 Prof. Mohammed Ismail 
 We want that education by which character is formed, strength of mind is increased, the intellect is expanded, and by which one can stand on one’s own feet” 
 Principal's Message 
 Today, there is a dynamic advancement in IT and Communication Technologies. The Institutions imparting Technical Education have to provide graduates with technical and life skills that facilitate a smooth transition from Institute to Industry and Academia. Sasi Institute of Technology and Engineering established in the year 2002 has come a long way, and is evolving by the day to catch up with the demanding market trends. It is an A+ NAAC accredited Institute with several departments accredited by NBA that is a guarantee for quality education. Students are provided with opportunities that facilitate their overall personality development. Regular interaction with industry experts is arranged through Guest Lectures, Industrial Visits. Situated in lush greenery, the Institute has well qualified staff, state of the art laboratories, well equipped spacious library and peaceful atmosphere that make learning becomes a wonderful experience. It as an ideal place parents can put their child in our safe hands.   
 Principal's Profile 
 Professor, Dept. of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Near Aerodrome, Tadepalligudem, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh- 534101 E-mail:  [email protected] Phone: +91-8217855759 scholar.google.com/citations?user=eSYI9_AAAAAJ hl=en authuser=1 Scopus ID: 57189343053 Prof. Mohammed Ismail had his early education in St. John’s High School and Wardlaw Junior College, Bellary, Karnataka. Then he pursued his B.E from Gulbarga University, M.Tech in Communication Systems from VTU, Belgaum, and Ph.D from VMU was awarded in 2010. He has 20 plus years of full time teaching experience in premier Engineering colleges in Bangalore and Hyderabad, including 7 years in Mekelle University, Ethiopia, a research centric university with 1200 th  position in international ranking. He is awarded with Professorship by the Mekelle University Board upon recommendations by the Mekelle University Senate, and is the first Indian to be awarded by the University in its nearly 30 year history. He is a Ph.D research supervisor for two students, one at Mekelle University-Addis Ababa- Hawk University of Applied Science and Arts, Germany, and the other is at Addis Ababa Institute of Technology, Ethiopia. He has guided 40 plus M.Tech(Research) students in diverse Antenna Technologies, and has published more than 20 technical papers in peer reviewed Journals including IEEE Transaction on Antenna and Propagation. Additionally, he has published articles related to ICT integration in teaching pedagogy and its impact on Learning and Employability. He is a reviewer for several SCIE/Scopus indexed peer reviewed Journals viz. International Journal of Microwave and Wireless technologies, International Journal of Communication Systems, Automotive and Vehicle Technology and Systems, Journals of Computing, International Journal of R.F and Microwave Computer Aided Engineering.

title: Sri M. Narendra Krishna - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Sri M. Narendra Krishna - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Vice Chairman 
 Sri M. Narendra Krishna 
 I take great pride in welcoming you to our campus. We assure a climate that encourages learning and personal growth. We value commitment to excellence in all we do. The aim of institution is to teach how to think, than what to think and how to learn than what to study. Education is the very way of our life and when it improves, life does too. Our motto is to provide a quality education to rural people which we are doing since 1980. Sasi Educational Institutes is recognized institution offering excellent school, college, undergraduate, graduate and professional education through 12 schools and colleges to nearly 16, 000 students. Sasians work every day to advance the common good in uncommon ways. We teach , We explore and We discover. We collaborate and lead. We innovate, inspire, and empower. We achieve our potential and create circumstances that help our students and others achieve theirs. .Our Founder’s words are inspiration to us ” No riches buy knowledge: but, knowledge owns any riches in the world.” 
 Vice Chairman Objectives 
 To improve the industry institute interaction To organize Conferences/Workshops/Seminars To promote R & D activity To strive for students’ placement To organize co-curricular and extra-curricular activities To improve personality of teaching & non-teaching staff To develop and maintain the linkage with alumni associations of SITE To create new facilities I know Sasi is still learning to leap. It has many heights to climb up. It has long distances to walk, But I assure you, with the co-operation and faith of that you have laid on us, that we would work for your best satisfaction during the times coming a head.

title: Guidelines for Student - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Guidelines for Student - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Guidelines For Student Registration For Courses 
 Student (newly admitted or on rolls) has to register for the course on the day of registration for each semester. Students failing to register for the course will not be permitted to attend classes. The College has the right to refuse registration process if a student does not turn up on the day of registration. Students will be permitted to register only if they have: Cleared all the fees, outstanding dues of College and / or Hostel of previous semesters, Paid all prescribed fees for the current semester, and Not been debarred from registering for a specified period on disciplinary or any other ground. It is the responsibility of the individual student to register for the semester. Normally, no late registration shall be permitted. However, considering any compelling reason, a student may be permitted for late registration (within one week of commencement of semester) with prior approval from the principal. Late registration may be done with payment of requisite fine. The College reserves the right to cancel the registration of a student from a course or semester or debar from the degree on disciplinary grounds. In each semester a student shall mandatorily register courses which he/she wishes to pursue within a week from the starting of the class work with the advice of Head of the Department and mentor of the student of the concerned department of the college. If any student wishes to withdraw the registration of the course, he/she shall submit a letter to the principal of the college through the Head of the Department and mentor within fifteen days. The concerned college shall thoroughly verify and upload the data/courses registered by each student in the university examination center within 20 days. The principal of the concerned college shall ensure that there no wrong registration courses by the student. The university registration portal will be closed after 20 days.

title: Alumni - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Alumni - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ALUMNI 
 Committee 
 SASI Alumni Association SASI has produced leaders, entrepreneurs, social entrepreneurs, artists, writers ,managers who collectively have a wealth of knowledge and experience. The SASI Alumni Association brings all these outstanding people together on a single platform. This collective excellence is our contribution to the growing generation, the Institute and the society as a whole. Engage, energize and enhance – keeping this motto in mind, we seamlessly connect with the Institute, faculty, students and fellow alumni. Goals The SASI Alumni Association aims to link the alumni to the institution, develop synergistic plans to support the institution and achieve its vision, and to enable the institute to add value to all its stakeholders. Our objectives are: To establish and maintain an alumni association structure – board, chapters, batch representatives that will enable in the smooth governance of the association. To inspire alumni to enhance the leadership of the Institute and stay connected To provide a focal point of contact among alumni to interact and network with each other To support the institute with resources that it will need for its various activities To strengthen ties between the Institute and alumni and encourage them to participate in the various cultural and social activities All graduates who have undergone programs from SASI including B.Tech, M.Tech, MCA, MBA programs above a certain duration automatically become eligible to be an Alumni Association member.

title: Student Reviews - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Student Reviews - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Pavuluri Vasundhara Devi 
 17K61A04F6 – Electronics & Communication Engineering 
 I am always glad to be a student at Sasi Institute of Technology and Engineering. Very disciplined college with a faculty of high educational standards and they teach us values along with the subject. They encourage us to indulge in various social and technical activities that are conducted annually and to participate in various workshops and competitions. I would like to thank the institute and my department for helping me to become what I am today. The faculty encouraged me all the time from the starting day to the end. I have improved my Personal and Professional skills such as self-confidence, strong communication and leadership skills. From the Infrastructure to Placements, everything was really good. Our college has a Central Library where we can get various books for gate exams, course related magazines too and can get benefit from it through gaining knowledge from those resources. We also have special classes added to our curriculum such as Soft Skills, English Communication Skills, and Aptitude which play prominent roles in transforming ourselves into professionals. Being an ECE student, I am provided with all the facilities related to core and what I am interested in. Placements are good at SITE; the institute provides very good training for campus placements. I am glad that I am placed in one of the reputed companies. In our college, we also have the RED ANTS club for enthusiastic young students who intend to learn something different in fields like photography, gardening, business, social service, culinary, arts, Athletics, Dance, singing etc to enhance our skills with our interest. We have KK consultancy which not only helps in internships but also encourages students to work after college hours as part time. They educate students to have a good balance in working and learning. We can get an idea of what we are good at. 
 M. Devendra Nath Chowdary 
 2017-2021 – Mechanical Engineering 
 My four years at the Sasi Institute of Technology and Engineering were great and a memory to cherish for a lifetime. It was full of learning and grooming oneself. It gave me an opportunity to meet different kinds of people and learn many things. I am thankful to all the faculties, mentors and the entire SITE as well as the placement cell. I got placed in Infosys campus placements from Mechanical Engineering. Overall, it was a great experience and lifetime memory over here. 
 Shaik Salman 
 17K61A0396 – Mechanical Engineering 
 I would like to thank all the members of SITE for their support each and every time when we needed it. Sasi Institute of Technology & Engineering believes in quality education with technical, practical, and social knowledge. Being a Mechanical engineering student, I saw proper and well-equipped laboratories, workshops, projects, and industrial visits. Studying at SITE has made me learn things very fast due to the enhanced learning process. Today I can proudly say that I’ve graduated from SITE as a Mechanical Engineer by understanding theoretical concepts, being able to apply them, and being ready to enter the professional world. Faculty members are dedicated and passionate about their work. The atmosphere of the college is very good. College creates good career opportunities for students. I got selected by three companies through campus selections in Accenture, Hitachi Consultancy and Ramtech. 
 K.D.S. Prakash 
 18K65A0329 – Mechanical Engineering 
 As we all know building our career is very important in our life. Many years pass away with different education. As I completed my secondary education I was familiar with some relative knowledge that how to take the correct path in our career. I was a Diploma passed out student and did my graduation from the Mechanical Dept, at Sasi Institute of Engineering & Technology, Tadepalligudem. When I was in the 2nd Year of engineering I was a bit different as compared to previous studies. The faculty members were very interactive with students and friendly, knowing their interests and thoughts. During those graduation days, I learned a lot of technical and conceptual knowledge as part of my academics. Faculty share their knowledge perfectly in an effective manner with the students. Later on faculty encouraged me to do technical projects and every aspect was supported, As part of my graduation I did a technical project based on a survey of physically challenged persons. I have come up with an idea as a model. During 3rd year have successfully participated in International Fest with the support of all the faculty members and achieved 1st place. During my graduation there was a lot of improvement in my day-to-day life exploring innovations and thoughts, I was very much thankful for each and every staff member being taken care of every personal. Compared to the starting day of my graduation till the end the improvement is very impressive and I can see myself in a good position. I completed my graduation with a CGPA of 8.4 as an academic topper. I thank each and every faculty for encouraging the students in all aspects such as Technical and academic programs. Every student needs good support for building a career in their life. Striving for success without hard work is like trying to harvest when you haven t planted. 
 B. Teja Venkata Sai 
 17K61A0312 – Mechanical Engineering 
 I am most thankful to the Institution for providing me the opportunity to better myself through education, communication, co-ordination, management and experience with the real-world situations by employing activities like social service activities, college fests, sports meet, cultural events, recycle art competition, debates and other extra-curricular activities which made us ready to face the real world. The care, support and guidance showcased by the faculty members of mechanical department was remarkable in every means. For me these four years of graduation had been a rollercoaster ride with a perfect blend of joy and hardships. 
 Sivaram Prasad 
 2008, 928 (GATE-2009) 
 I believe that I made the right decision by choosing the Sasi Institute of Technology & Engineering College. I am grateful to my faculty and management for their help and support throughout my journey during my graduation. They guided me in every way they could. An assortment of technical workshops, paper presentations & seminars are organized by the institute to provide us the knowledge of current trends of engineering industries. The hostel authority gave personal attention to each and every student to excel in their discipline and academics. Really it s great to have such good, caring and supportive mentors. . Library facilities were helped me a lot to reach my dreams on academics and higher education. Specifically in my case, I should thank the faculty members of ECE department for spending their time to clear the concepts during my GATE preparation. The motivation given by Mr Ravi Shankar sir for GATE preparation gave me a lifelong inspiration and confidence. After my graduation, I completed my M.Tech from NIT-Calicut and secured the University Gold medal. Finally I advise my juniors to utilize the resources provided by the institute as much as possible at right time.My self is SIVARAM PRASAD M(2005-09 Batch,ECE) , Engineer, Kalki Communications and Technologies Pvt Ltd. 
 B.Srinivas 
 2008, 928 (GATE-2009) 
 Student life starts with A , B , C .But a professional life starts with graduate studies as technicalities and personal skills. We are so lucky to have such a great opportunity in our college. College is maintaining high standards in giving right input to the student in the right time. In addition to the regular classes, we will be having seasonal classes in the campus for the placements, higher studies and PSU exams. During summer we have placement sessions by the professionals from well-known organisations and by the experienced faculty. These will help a student to improve his attitude and personal skills. Each dept. has the separate gate coaching co-ordinates for the interested students to improve their technical skills. These will help the students to clear the competitive tests like GATE, IES, ISRO, DRDO and all public sector exams. Am the best example for this, I have best advices during my preparation for the GATE exam those only help me to clear it. With those skills am doing my Ph.D in IIT MADRAS [IITM]. My self is B.SRINIVAS, 2005-09 ECE batch. AGGREGATE: 78.2 % GATE RANKS: 1691 (GATE

title: Higher Education - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Higher Education - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Opportunities after B.Tech It is necessary for one to decide their career. Now that you have entered into B.Tech. So, now you have to start looking into your future.   Study or Job There are two options you prefer after you complete your B.Tech degree. If you are economically efficient and dont need a job, it is better you go for your further studies. A specialization will always earn you more salary as well as priority during interviews and of course sound grip over subject. Otherwise you can find some job.   Further Studies If you prefer to continue to studies, you can do Post graduate courses and then obtain a Ph.D. You have lots of options during this P.G course like M.S/ M.Tech, IAS/IPS, Navy/NDA and Finance related courses like MBA. M.S/M.Tech:  Entrance exams for Post Graduation in Engineering Courses. If you prefer doing P.G in your field of study, you can go for M.S/M.Tech. If you are ready to go outside India and financially sound you can prefer M.S. You need to appear for GRE/TOEFL for doing M.S in Foreign countries. GRE:  The Graduate Record Examination or GRE is a standardized test that is an admissions requirement for many graduate schools in English speaking countries. It is created and administered by the Educational Testing Service and is similar in format and content to the SAT. It is a computer based Online Test. The percentile scored in this exam will decide your future in doing M.S in foreign nations. TOEFL:  The Test of English as a Foreign Language (or TOEFL, pronounced “toe-full” or sometimes “toffle”) evaluates the potential success of an individual to use and understand Standard American English at a college level. It is required for non-native applicants at many English-speaking colleges and universities. A TOEFL score is valid for two years and then is deleted from the official database. Since its introduction in late 2005, the Internet-based test (iBT) has progressively replaced both the computer-based (CBT) and paper-based (PBT) tests. The iBT has been introduced in phases, with the United States, Canada, France, Germany, and Italy in 2005 and the rest of the world in 2006, with test centers added regularly. The demand for test seats remains very high even after almost a year after the introduction of the test: Candidates have to wait for months since short-term test dates are fully booked. The four-hour test consists of four sections, each measuring mainly one of the basic language skills (although some tasks may require multiple skills) and focusing on language used in an academic, higher-education environment. Note-taking is allowed in the iBT. GATE: Conducted by one of seven Indian Institutes of Technology in rotation, Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) is an annual exam for admission to M.Tech and M.S. programmes in most engineering institutes in India[citation needed]. It is regarded as a benchmark test for engineering graduates in India [citation needed]. This examination is coordinated by a committee, comprising of Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore and seven Indian Institutes of Technology on behalf of the National Coordinating Board – GATE, Department of Education, and Government of India. The pattern and syllabus are usually based on a candidate’s B.Tech. Or BE syllabus. Minimum eligibility for appearing in this exam is usually a B.Tech, BE, B. Arch. or Masc. The exam is usually conducted on second Sunday of February. PSU’s like BARC, NPCIL, HAL etc. give importance to GATE scores for various job positions in their organizations.

title: Center For Innovation & Entrepreneurship - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Center For Innovation & Entrepreneurship - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Overview 
 SASI Institute for Engineering and Technology, Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (SASI CIE) is established for fostering Innovation, promoting Entrepreneurship, and providing a supportive ecosystem for startups. Rooted in a commitment to converting innovative technologies into successful commercial ventures, we provide a robust platform for aspiring entrepreneurs in the dynamic landscape of contemporary business world for growth, knowledge-sharing, and the creation of a vibrant entrepreneurial community within the SASI CIE ecosystem. 
 Vision Statement 
 To emerge as the preeminent centre of excellence for Innovation and Entrepreneurship in Andhra Pradesh by 2028 and be among the top 10 establishments nationally.  Mission Statement: To provide a nurturing culture of creativity, resilience, and continuous learning. To create a vibrant hub where innovation thrives propelling the institute to the forefront of the entrepreneurial landscape through strategic collaboration, mentorship, and access to resources. Empower individuals and enterprises to realize their full potential, driving economic prosperity and societal impact. 
 Objectives 
 SASI Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CIE) serves as a dynamic hub dedicated to fostering creativity, problem-solving, and entrepreneurial thinking among students, faculty, and staff. CIE objectives are Cultivate Innovation Encourage Ideation Facilitate Entrepreneurial Development Provide Mentorship Drive Economic Growth Create Jobs, Wealth, and Business Aligning with National Priorities Promote New Technology/Knowledge/Innovation-Based Startups Provide a Platform for Speedy Commercialization of Technologies Build a Vibrant Startup Ecosystem Provide Cost-Effective, Value-Added Services 
 Activities 
 Promotes Creativity:  Encourages and facilitates creative thinking among students and faculty through workshops, brainstorming sessions, and innovation challenges. Supports Entrepreneurship:  Provides resources, mentorship, and networking opportunities to aspiring entrepreneurs within the institution, guiding them through the process of ideation, prototyping, and launching ventures. Facilitates Collaboration:  Creates platforms for interdisciplinary collaboration, bringing together individuals from various departments and backgrounds to collaborate on innovative projects and initiatives. Offers Training and Workshops:  Organizes training sessions, seminars, and workshops on topics such as design thinking, lean startup methodologies, and emerging technologies to equip participants with the skills and knowledge necessary for innovation and entrepreneurship. Incubates Startups:  Provides incubation support to promising startups founded by students, faculty, or alumni, offering access to workspace, funding opportunities, mentorship, and networking resources to help them grow and succeed. Fosters Industry Partnerships:  Establishes partnerships with industry leaders, startups, and innovation ecosystems to facilitate technology transfer, collaborative research, and internship opportunities for students. Promotes Innovation Culture:  Cultivates a culture of innovation and risk-taking within the institution by celebrating and recognizing achievements, fostering a supportive environment for experimentation, and promoting a mindset of continuous improvement. Drives Research and Development:  Encourages faculty and students to pursue research and development projects that address real-world challenges and opportunities, supporting them in securing funding, patents, and commercialization pathways for their innovations. Engages with the Community:  Collaborates with the local community, government agencies, and non-profit organizations to address societal needs, stimulate economic development, and promote social innovation through collaborative projects and initiatives. Measures Impact:  Tracks and evaluates the impact of its programs and initiatives on fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic growth within the institution and the broader community, using metrics such as startup success rate, patents filed, and jobs created. 
 Incubation 
 Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CIE) provides a platform for potential startups into accelerating their products. We provide support and facilities for startups at various stages to grow and reach full potential. MSME Business Incubator Funded by MSME, Govt of India, which aims to promote and inculcate spirit of Entrepreneurship. NewGEN IEDC funded by DTS Govt of India, promotes and facilitates the development of ideas into innovative tangible products. SASI Incubation Centre is self-funded and dedicated to fostering a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship. Our goal is to provide comprehensive support for every stage of innovation, from ideation to commercialization, across products, services, and processes. We help with developing prototypes, gaining early traction in the market, and facilitating commercialization efforts. 
 Facilities 
 Furnished A/C office space/open co-working space. Meeting and conference rooms Video Conferencing Auditorium/conference halls for large events Computers up to two and extra on rental basis. High Speed Internet service Access to Host institute Laboratory and Research Facilities Access to networking Zones Prototyping and Testing facilities IPR support services Scanner and Printer Phone Connection Security Water Plug and play. Access to Green spaces and Recreation areas. 
 Services 
 Technical support by Experts Startup India MAARG mentors support Marketing and market analysis Assistance Comprehensive Business development Assistance Funding support / access to network of investors, funding institutions etc Information on various funding sources/programs available Financial, Accounting, legal and Compliance Assistance Startup India Recognition DPIIT assistance Access to various Startup India, Government and Non-government programs Information on events, hackathons, ideathons etc all-round the year Access to networking events and Investor events Access to training, mentoring and workshops. 
 Funding 
 Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CIE) understand that turning ideas into successful businesses requires more than just creativity—it also requires robust financial support. That’s why we offer a range of funding facilities to nurture and empower aspiring entrepreneurs and startups. Our funding options include seed funding for early-stage ventures, mentorship-backed grants to fuel growth, and access to venture capital networks for scalable enterprises. We also aid in navigating grant programs and connecting with angel investors and strategic partners. At our Incubation Centre, we believe in providing not just physical space but also the financial resources needed for your ideas to thrive and make a meaningful impact in the market. We have successfully received funding support from MSME and NewGEN IEDC to support incubation and help turn innovative ideas into tangible products that have made significant impact in the market by way of patents and commercialization. 
 I&E Policy 
 SASI NISP policy 
 Contact 
 M S Pavankumar Raju Head Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship Sasi Institute of Technology and Engineering email:  [email protected]

title: About SITE R&D - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
About SITE R&D - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 Research and Development (R&D) Cell at SASI Institute of Technology& Engineering plays a pivotal role in fostering and coordinating research initiatives aimed at addressing societal challenges. The cell actively manages and facilitates funding and consultancy projects, thereby enhancing the research capabilities of both faculty and students. By engaging collaborative efforts with industries and premier funding agencies, the institute actively contributes to solving real-world problems. In its pursuit of turning vision and mission into reality, the institution take proactive steps to promote innovations among faculty and students. Through strategic partnerships with industries, the institute seeks mutual benefits and actively participates in problem-solving endeavours. Committed to achieving excellence in both fundamental research and the development of cutting-edge technologies, the institution provides a high-quality research training experience for its students. To stay at the forefront of global research, the institute maintains relevance by establishing connections with industry and academia. This is accomplished through active participation in local and international research networks, ensuring a continuous exchange of knowledge and ideas.

title: NSS - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
NSS - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 OBJECTIVES 
 ACTIVITIES & REPORTS 
 Case Studies 
 Awards & Recognitions 
 Contact 
 ABOUT 
 The National Service Scheme, also known as NSS, is an extension of activities to the higher education system that orients student youth to community service while they are studying in educational institutions under the auspices of the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India.NSS (National Service Scheme) Unit of SITE to make the students aware of social responsibilities and dignity of labor and make them self reliant. The programme aims to inculcate social welfare in students, and to provide service to society without bias. NSS volunteers work to ensure that everyone who is needy gets help to enhance their standard of living and lead a life of dignity. In doing so, volunteers learn from people in villages how to lead a good life despite a scarcity of resources. it also provides help in natural and man-made disasters by providing food, clothing and first aid to the disaster victims. 
 OBJECTIVES 
 Understand the community in which they work Understand themselves in relation to their community Identify the needs and problems of the community and involve them in problem-solving Develop among themselves a sense of social and civic responsibility Utilize their knowledge in finding practical solutions to individual and community problems Develop competence required for group-living and sharing of responsibilities Gain skills in mobilizing community participation Acquire leadership qualities and democratic attitudes Develop capacity to meet emergencies and natural disasters and practice national integration and social harmony Motto The Motto of NSS “Not Me But You”, reflects the essence of democratic living and upholds the need for self-less service. NSS helps the students develop appreciation to other person’s point of view and also show consideration to other living beings. The philosophy of the NSS is well doctrine in this motto, which underlines/on the belief that the welfare .of an individual is ultimately dependent on the welfare of the society on the whole and therefore, the NSS volunteers shall strive for the well-being of the society. 
 ACTIVITIES & REPORTS 
 2023-24 2022-23 2021-22 2020-21 2019-20 2018-19 
 Case Studies 
 Knowledge and Attitude Profile (WK) WK1 A systematic, theory-based understanding of the natural sciences applicable to the discipline and awareness of relevant social sciences. WK2 Conceptually-based mathematics, numerical analysis, data analysis, statistics and formal aspects of computer and information science to support detailed analysis and modelling applicable to the discipline. WK3 A systematic, theory-based formulation of engineering fundamentals required in the engineering discipline. WK4 Engineering specialist knowledge that provides theoretical frameworks and bodies of knowledge for the accepted practice areas in the engineering discipline; much is at the forefront of the discipline. WK5 Knowledge, including efficient resource use, environmental impacts, whole-life cost, re- use of resources, net zero carbon, and similar concepts, that supports engineering design and operations in a practice area. WK6 Knowledge of engineering practice (technology) in the practice areas in the engineering discipline. WK7 Knowledge of the role of engineering in society and identified issues in engineering practice in the discipline, such as the professional responsibility of an engineer to public safety and sustainable development. WK8 Engagement with selected knowledge in the current research literature of the discipline, awareness of the power of critical thinking and creative approaches to evaluate emerging issues. WK9 Ethics, inclusive behavior and conduct. Knowledge of professional ethics, responsibilities, and norms of engineering practice. Awareness of the need for diversity by reason of ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability etc. with mutual understanding and respect, and of inclusive attitudes. Program Outcomes: PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4) PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5) PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8). PO5 Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6) PO6 The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7). PO7 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9) PO8 Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO9 Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences PO10 Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments. PO11 Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

title: Women Welfare/Sexual harassment Eradication cell - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Women Welfare/Sexual harassment Eradication cell - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 FUNCTIONS 
 ACTIVITIES 
 Contact 
 ABOUT 
 The Women Welfare / Sexual harassment eradication Cell of the College has been established to safeguard the self-esteem and rights of the women in the college. The women redressal consists of seven faculty members and fifteen student members. The cell attends to various complaints from the women in the campus, especially to issues regarding women harassment. Appropriate and prompt actions are assured to complainants approaching the cell. Vision The foremost objective of this cell is to safeguard the self-esteem and rights of the women in the college; empowering them to take part in all the activities of the college and thereby make them to feel that they are no longer inferior to men. Women’s Welfare Cell renders its support for the upliftment of girl students of rural areas. Women’s Welfare Cell also provides a platform to uphold the dignity of women at work. Any type of sexual harassment ie physical, verbal or mental shall come under the purview of the cell, and it is empowered to initiate legal actions against such offences. Mission To enlighten the women in the college,about their legal rights and to strive towards the empowerment of the women through the promotion of gender amity and programmes concerning women welfare. 
 FUNCTIONS 
 Women’s Empowerment through Education. Solving women related issues and complains. Creating awareness about women’s welfare and conducting Programmes concerning women’s welfare. Handle case works and counseling. Organizing the Self-defense Course for the women/girls of the college community. Improving interpersonal skill. Creating awareness against harassment. Promotion of Gender amity Organize health awareness programmes such as stress-control or prevention of cancer. Organize seminar/workshops on legal rights of women. Organize cultural programmes with the basic objective of increasing awareness in women. 
 ACTIVITIES 
 Click Here

title: Anti Ragging - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Anti Ragging - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 OBJECTIVES 
 FUNCTIONS 
 CONTACT 
 ABOUT 
 It is a body at institutional level to establish measures for prohibiting, preventing and banning activities of Ragging Menace within and outside the campus in accordance with  AICTE/UGC  Regulations, supreme court directives and state act, the committee is responsible for taking action against those found guilty of ragging and or abetting ragging, actively or passively or being part of a conspiracy to promote ragging. 
 OBJECTIVES 
 Creating ragging free atmosphere in and outside the campus. Creating awareness among students regarding acts and punishments pertaining to ragging activities. Creating cordial relations among students for fulfilling organizations missions and objectives. Creating awareness among students regarding how the self confidence of the individual influences the national interest. 
 FUNCTIONS 
 To uphold and comply with the directions of the Hon’ble supreme court and be vigilant on any acts amounting to ragging. To publicize to all students and prevalent directives and the actions that can be taken against those indulging in ragging. To consider the complaints received from the students and conduct enquiry and submit report to the principal along with punishment recommended for the offenders. From anti-ragging committees/squads to make surprise visits and takes effective steps to prevent ragging. Oversee the procedure of obtaining undertaking from the students in accordance with the provisions. Conduct workshops against ragging menace and orient the students. To provide students the information pertaining to contact address and telephone numbers of the person (s) identified to receive complaints/distress calls. To offer services of counseling and create awareness to the students. To take all necessary measures for prevention of ragging inside the campus/Hostels. The committee shall monitor the functioning of the vigilance committee and vigilance anti-ragging squad. 
 CONTACT 
 B. Kiran Kumar Coordinator, Anti Ragging Committee, Department of Mechanical Engineering, SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering, Tadepalligudem- 534101 Email id : [email protected]

title: Central Library - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Central Library - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 About 
 About Library The Central Library is housed in an exclusive two storied building. The Circulation Section, Stack Area (Text Book Section), E-Learning Centre  (Digital Library), Journals Section, Reprographic Room and Binding Room are accommodated on the ground floor with more than 288 seating capacity. The Reference Section, Back Volumes, Project Reports, Discussion Rooms, Internet Centre /Video Room and separate toilets for both ladies and gents are located on the first floor. GROUND FLOOR FIRST FLOOR Ø  Circulation Section Ø  Stack Area (Text Book Section) Ø  E-Learning Centre  (Digital Library) Ø  Journals Section Ø  Reprographic Room Ø  Binding Section Ø  Reference Section Ø  Back Volumes Ø  Project Reports Ø  Discussion Rooms Ø  Internet Centre/Video Room Ø  Toilets

NPTEL Online courses Local Chapter link https://nptel.ac.in/localchapter/statistics/516 1000+ Video & Web Courses 9. All Depts. Library Website https://sitecentrallibrary.wordpress.com/ Everything about Library Resources & Services IEEE, ASCE, ASME , Springer Journals & DELNET access provided on IP based access For N-List & NDLI (Individual accounts are created and send the details to email) Please feel free to contact Librarian/Library staff if you need any further assistance. 
 Plagiarism Checking Service 
 Plagiarism Checking Service – Using DrillBit Tool The Library offers a comprehensive Plagiarism Checking Service through the DrillBit tool, available to the students, faculty, and staff of SITE. This service ensures that academic and research work is free from plagiarism and meets the highest standards of originality. About DrillBit: DrillBit is a leading plagiarism detection and similarity checking tool that allows users to compare their work against vast databases, including: Academic journals, Research papers, Internet content previous student submissions.  It ensures that any similarities with existing content are identified, helping researchers maintain academic integrity before final submission. Who Can Use the Service : Students: For checking assignments, research papers, dissertations, and theses. Faculty and Researchers: For journal articles, manuscripts, project reports, and other scholarly work. Staff: For academic and administrative reports or any official documents.   How to Use the Service: To have your document checked for plagiarism and receive a detailed similarity report, follow these simple steps: Email your request to: [email protected] Attach the document to be checked, which should be in PDF or DOCX format. Accepted documents include: Theses Dissertations Manuscripts of journal articles Research papers Project reports Assignments Wait for the report: After submitting your document, the library team will process it and email the similarity report back to you. Benefits of Using the DrillBit Tool: Comprehensive Analysis: The tool checks across multiple sources and databases to ensure no matching or copied content is missed. Detailed Report: You’ll receive a report highlighting similar content, with percentage matches for each section. Academic Integrity: This service helps maintain ethical standards in research and education by detecting unintentional plagiarism. For further information or assistance, please feel free to reach out to the library staff. 
 General Rules 
 General Rules Producing the ID card is mandatory. Users should maintain strict silence in the library. Use of mobiles is prohibited in the library. Members will not write or mark on the books belonging to the library. Members shall not shelve books or periodicals that will disturb the regular order. Members are responsible for any damage caused to the books or any other property belonging to the library and will be liable to pay the penalty according to the rules. The members caught tearing pages/stealing of books will be suspended forthwith from using the library facilities and further disciplinary action will be initiated against them. Users don t bring personal belongings (including books and other printed materials) and also the books already borrowed from the library. Members need to check their materials at the exist/check point while leaving the library. The Librarian reserves the right to suspend the membership of any member found misbehaving, abusing the library staff or behaving in an indecent manner. Circulation Situated at the entrance of the Library and divided into three divisions viz. Property Counter Security Counter Circulation Counter Property Counter:  Members are advised to keep their personal belongings including handbags, printed materials, etc. at the property counter placed at the both sides of the entrance. Security Counter:  Members have to show their identity card at the security desk and sign in the gate register at the time of entering and leaving the library. Book(s) issued on loan and all permitted materials like note books, files, etc. taken inside the library are to be given for inspection by the security staff when leaving the library. Circulation Counter: The circulation counter is automated. Books are issued on loan and received returns in this counter. The maximum number of books that can be borrowed is available in the Identity Card. Barcode scanners are used in the circulation counter for issue/return of books. The books that are not returned by due date, the overdue charges of Rs.2/- per day will be collected at the counter. Books can be borrowed against the ID card with individual presence. Library ensures the elimination of defective / damaged books. Any users find a defective book/ damaged book, bring it to the notice of circulation staff. Damaged / defective books returned by the users are charged as per the rules. Circulation Policy General books are issued to all eligible members as per their entitlements. The renewal limit is one time, subject to the condition that it will not be requested by another user. A user can reserve a book which is on loan by using OPAC. Transfer of books is not permissible. Books issued will not be accepted back on the same day Loss of book must be reported immediately. Late fees, if any, will be charged till the loss of the book is reported. If the original library book is returned after reporting it lost, the fine will be calculated till the date of its return. 
 Dept Libraries 
 Department Libraries Besides the Central Library, every Department has its own departmental library. The Library supports all the individual Department Libraries for acquisition and maintenance of titles. title: SITE – SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
SITE – SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 SITE - SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter (LC ID: 516) 
 Established the SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter in association with IIT Madras in the month of March 2016 Having access to 1000+ web and video courses of SWAYAM NPTEL – view, download and copy of Offline Videos are available. The objective of enabling students to obtain certificates from courses is to make students employable in the industry or pursue a suitable higher education program. Avail 50% exam fees waiver option  for LC students* (as per the guidelines of NPTEL) Access to certificate exam resources exclusive for LC (Hall tickets, Results & Certificates etc.,) 
 SITE SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter link: https://nptel.ac.in/LocalChapter/statistics/516/ To access NPTEL Resources: https://nptel.ac.in/ NPTEL GATE Preparation Portal: https://gate.nptel.ac.in/ SPOC Details: Mr. K Srinu Librarian & SPOC SWAYAM NPTEL Local Chapter, Dept of Library & Information Science, Email: [email protected] , Cell: 9491372286

title: Physical Education - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Physical Education - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 GYMNASIUM 
 FACILITIES 
 ACTIVITIES 
 AWARDS 
 CONTACT 
 ABOUT 
 Department of Physical Education aims to develop physical and mental strength of students and make them competitive in their personal and professional life. Ever since its inception in 2002, the department provides an excellent coaching and training facilities in sports and games. The qualified faculty trains the students regularly in Athletics, Volleyball, Table Tennis, Chess, Kabaddi, Yoga, Throw Ball, Cricket, Kho-Kho, and Badminton. Nearly 125 sport students have yielded outstanding results at all levels including national level. The college teams participate in all intercollegiate and other major sports activities at University and National level. Some of the students have gained recognition at the University level and National level events. The best sporting facilities and intensive sport programs are being conducted by time to time for the holistic development of our students. Objectives To improve physical fitness and strength To improve competitive spirit Motivate the students to involve in physical activities and sports Feeling the sense of wellness of the students and staff by participating in sports To make the students participate in the tournaments and loyalty to the college To develop leadership quality and overall development by involving in sports 
 GYMNASIUM 
 There is a full-fledged gymnasium that is maintained by qualified trainers who train for students towards including the endurance level that is vital for success is sports field and helpful towards professional growth. 
 FACILITIES 
 The deep red colored clay courts and cricket fields are being maintained to conduct competitions for students and  faculty from time to time. Details of Outdoor Facilities S.No. Description 1 200Mts Track 2 Cricket Play Ground 3 Throw Ball 4 Badminton Courts 5 Volley Ball Courts 6 Cricket Net Practice 7 Kabaddi Courts 8 Kho-Kho courts 9 Tennicoit courts 10 Ball Badminton Details of Indoor Facilities S.No. Description 1 Chess 2 Caroms 3 Table Tennis 4 Yoga Centre 
 ACTIVITIES 
 Preparation of play fields for the use of players for time to time. Conduct of selection trails to pick up talented players for participation at inter collegiate tournaments at JNTUK, Kakinada, practice matches and invitation tournaments of students and staff. Coaching provided to the participating teams. Providing games and sports material for the regular practice. To tone up general fitness purpose, Gymnasium equipment of 18 individual stations available for the students. Calling quotations for the purchase of quality sports material Attending university tournaments and general body meetings of JNTUK University. Organizing practice matches for different events from time to time Sending outstanding players for participation at National level events, tournaments and also for the coaching camps. Guiding students/players towards academic accomplishments Providing sports uniform for the students for participation in tournaments. Financial support to the outstanding sports person to participate in international/national level tournaments. Conducting intramural games and sports competitions for men and women students separately in volleyball, cricket, table tennis, badminton, throwball, ball badminton, Tennicoit, carrom, chess and athletics events. Conducting games and sports competitions for staff of the college both teaching and non teaching (men and women seperately) Presenting merit certificates and mementos to the winners and runners in the college competitions. Providing TA/DA to the players for participation in the outside tournaments. Grant of attendance to the participating students in the competitions. 
 AWARDS 
 For Awards / Participation details Click here 
 CONTACT

title: Hostel - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Hostel - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 ACTIVITIES 
 FACILITIES 
 INSTRUCTIONS 
 CONTACT 
 ABOUT 
 SITE has separate hostel facility for boys and girls. The hostel is located in the college premises itself. The mess is spacious with a dining hall and kitchen. Separate seating arrangements are made for both boys and girls. Full time resident wardens are available to take care of hostellers and to maintain the discipline. A standby power generator is available with full time attendant in the hostel to operate during power failures. Life in hostel campus helps the students not only to study but also to socialize. SASI provides a comfortable and safe accommodation to all its students. The students accommodated in the campus are encouraged to use the gymnasium, playground and all indoor gaming facilities. The hostel, in addition, has a general store for provisions required by the students. Study hours will be conducted exclusively to the hostellers after college hours to improve their academic performance. 
 ACTIVITIES 
 To build an all-round personality, the Hostel students are actively involved in a large number of co-curricular and extra-curricular activities. Hostel Premier League (HPL) is conducted every year “Seminar Junction” an event for seminar sessions which are conducted in week ends to improve communication skills Short films contests are conducted to encourage the creativity in the hostellers 
 FACILITIES 
 Fully furnished and spacious room Study hours R O mineral water round the clock 100% hygienic and quality food First aid facility Uninterrupted supply of water and electricity. Round the clock security guard Provision and general stores Coin telephones Under senior faculty supervision Indoor games Availability of Electricians and Plumber Assistant Wardens are available for consultation and guidance for the benefit of the students. The Central Bank, ATM is operational on the campus. Computer Lab Green carpet lawn 
 INSTRUCTIONS 
 Every ward has to pay, the entire fee prescribed by the Hostel Authorities before admitting to the hostel. Inmate has to maintain discipline in all respects. Inmate should not leave the hostel without prior permission of the Hostel In Charge/Warden. Any damage / misbehavior made by the inmate to hostel / mess property will attract the fine. General breakage charges will be collected from every inmate at the end of the academic year / Semester for issuing the hall ticket by the concerned institute. Use of cell phones, smoking, illegal activities and teasing the inmates is prohibited in the campus. Individual Inmates are responsible for their belongings. Inmates who want to use the stationary / book stores under credit bases has to pay 5,000/- as caution deposit. Otherwise, they have to pay cash every time they purchase required material. 
 CONTACT 
 Dr. Mohammed Rafee Hostel Incharge SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering Tadepalligudem- 534101 Email id : [email protected]

title: Canteen - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Canteen - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ABOUT 
 OBJECTIVE 
 CONTACT 
 ABOUT 
 The College has a well established canteen in the campus. It forms the junction of the students from different streams/courses. It makes all edible items available to the students and faculty members as per their requirement. The environment in the canteen is clean & hygienic. The rates charged for various items of food are checked and approved by the college administration. Canteen is open on working days from 8.30am to 5.30pm. A canteen committee is responsible for monitoring the operations of the canteen and implementing and reviewing the canteen policy. They may also be responsible for specific decisions as specified in the canteen policy, such as employing staff, authorising major purchases or authorizing changes in the menu. A canteen committee is a proven way to give the canteen enough autonomy to operate as a business while maintaining strong connections with the institute. Some advantages of having a canteen committee are: The workload of overseeing the canteen can be shared amongst a team. It is a forum where different views and ideas from the organization can be considered without disrupting the day to day management of the canteen. It separates the direction of the canteen from the management of the canteen, giving the organization more flexibility in how the canteen operates. 
 OBJECTIVE 
 Objective: To monitor the operations of the canteen, Implementing and reviewing the canteen policies.   Functions: To plan, organize, control and identify the needs of the canteen and also recommend any changes or facilities to make canteen the best hangout in campus. To prepare canteen menu and price list. To plan and allocate separate space for staff and also ensure safe, healthy, hygienic atmosphere at the canteen. 
 CONTACT 
 Dr. P Hema Chandu Coordinator, Canteen /House Keeping/Hygiene/Sanitation, SASI Institute of Technology & Engineering, Tadepalligudem- 534101 Email id : [email protected]

title: Red Ants - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
Red Ants - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 About 
 RED ANTS:  An Initiative towards building multiple career skills The  RED ANTS  (Research, Entrepreneurship and Development, A New Trend in Sasi/SITE) is An Initiative towards building multiple career skills to extend support to the students in moulding their interests in Extra-curricular activities and life skills. 
 Objectives 
 Social Service Club: To serve the society in its possible need and perform the responsibilities to the society.   Photography Club: To create the opportunities for students on Nature photography To excel their skills in Photography techniques through Workshops To give a chance into imagine, capture and freeze a moment and create beauty Coding Club: To increase coding literacy through KK Consultancy.  To create Logical thinking among engineering students.  To develop the skill employment for the campus placement.  To remove code phobia. Health Club: To accord high priority to physical and mental fitness. To encourage and adopt policies and practices towards fitness and well-being. To create awareness about the Health status and Health related issues among students and faculty members.     English Club: To Increase fluency, reduce accents and build confidence to think and speak English. Allow opportunity for students to express opinions and debate issues and thereby encourage critical thinking.   Arts & Crafts Club: To encourage creative expression, create artwork around campus, allow students to discover new crafts and bring together a group of like-minded people. Culinary Club: To emphasize the skills of students in preparing, cooking, and arranging healthy food To know the health benefits of food items Entrepreneurship Club: To help students to cultivate a startup business idea, teach them basic strategies like estimating costs and writing a business plan, and help them network their ideas toward sources of funding. Dance, Singing, Theater Arts: To boost up the morale of students and to highlight the talent of dance and creativity. To provide an opportunity to prove their talent and it is a good platform for them to have their artistic exploration. Gardening Club: To provide opportunities for the enjoyment of gardening To collect and disseminate information pertaining to horticulture. To provide a platform where the students can put their innovative efforts in organic cultivation of vegetables, fruits and flowers Cultural and Hobby Club: To foster creativity, cultural appreciation, and skill development in students. To provide a platform for talent exploration, builds confidence, and offers stress relief through engaging activities.

title: UBA Cell - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
UBA Cell - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 UNNAT BHARAT ABHIYAN 
 Official Correspondence 
 Click here 
 About UBA 
 Unnat Bharat Abhiyan (UBA)  is motivated by the idea of bringing about transformative change in rural development processes by using institutions knowledge to assist in the creation of an inclusive India’s architecture. The goal is to establish a virtuous loop between society and an open academic system by offering knowledge and techniques for growing professions, as well as to improve the skills of both the public and private sectors in responding to rural India’s development requirements. The current policy environment encourages national entities to adopt villages and develop relevant technology. UBA’s participating institute (PI) is Sasi Institute of Technology & Engineering (A) under the guidance of Regional coordinating institute (RCI) Andhra University, Visakhapatnam. Mr. Sk. Salman Basha, Assistant Professor, is the Coordinator of the UBA Cell, SITE (A), Tadepalligudem. The  SITE UBA Cell  has assembled an inter-disciplinary team of faculty and support staff. UBA allows students and faculty to redirect their energies toward measurable objectives. We are mandated to work through the Institute’s network of available units and will also attempt to cooperate with other community stakeholders. For example, the SITE UBA Cell will collaborate with NSS, NCC units, Community Service (CS) and other departments. We have identified a few villages near our campus and have opted to work on specific themes. We intend to build model villages surrounding our campus and then expand them up throughout the West Godavari district of Andhra Pradesh. SITE UBA & CE department has established Vermi Composting Unit used as fertilizers for organic farming. It is benefitted to increase the crop yield and reduce the usage of chemical fertilizers’. Farmers are invited from adopted villages and awareness programmes will be conducted to create awareness on importance of organic farming. SITE will work through the network of available units in the Institute and also try to collaborate with various stakeholders. SITE requesting all faculty members to make sure that 10-20 percent of all B.Tech project should be done with focus on inclusive rural development.  
 Areas of Intervention 
 Organic farming Literacy improvement programmes Best practices in aquaculture Women Empowerment Energy resources Drinking water quality Health Welfare Solid Waste management Skill development

title: ICT-ITL Integration Policy for Smart Evaluation - India's Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh
text:
ICT-ITL Integration Policy for Smart Evaluation - India s Best Private College in Andhra Pradesh 
 Close 
 ICT-ITL Integration Policy for Smart Evaluation Presently SASI is developing around 28 courses for JNTU-Kakinada under the coordinator ship of Dr. Sohel Rana to be floated on portal by JNTU-K & APSCHE which will be accessed by the students of the entire state of Andhra Pradesh. Apart from this SASI has implemented innovative teaching learning policy. Motivation: Assessment is process oriented; Evaluation is product oriented As per the instructions of Principal, and inputs from Director, with consultation of Dean-Academics, a new format proposed by Dean-ITL & ICT for innovative activities is being implemented. Inputs are collected from all the HoDs and suggestions given by AS&H HoD are incorporated in the proposed system. For improving the quality of outcome-based education it is necessary to involve students in the teaching learning process to the next level. As per the requirement of NBA and OBE a new policy for internal assessment of assignments is approved. A detailed workshop/faculty orientation as part of SRP will be conducted by the office of Dean (ITL & ICT). As all of us are aware that, present internal assessment for 30 marks is as follows: 
 Table-1 (Existing system) 
 These are all direct  methods of evaluation . We propose to involve  the direct/indirect method of assessment  as follows: 
 Table-2 (Proposed system) 
 Active learning Method-1 (E-content: Mandatory) Following are the steps for the ALM-1: Step-1 :  Record the class video while taking class or on laptop using quality PPTs and proper mic/camera stand etc. Ask assignment questions in the video lecture and don’t disclose them to students since they have to find the questions while watching video and upload the answers in real time. Step-2:  Upload the video on YouTube Step-3:  Prepare an online Google form with combination of quiz questions as well descriptive questions and paste the link in the description of video. Sample google form content is given in Appendix-2. Step-4:  Submit video link to Dean- (ITL & ICT)/HOD/ or Director for quality approval. Step-5 : After approval of competent authority share the video link to upload on the college website. Step-6 : Share the same uploaded video with students including assignment questions hidden in video. (Announce the time of sharing video in class with the consent of students) Step-7:  Announce the time in which students shall watch the video and make notes to upload in the Google link provided in video description (Sample format to be made after completion of activities is attached herewith as appendix-3) Sample video link with quiz + assignment link pasted in the description of the video is given below: 
 Google form link as given in the description of the video: 
 Click here 
 Benefits: One video from each faculty will be available on college website with proof of innovative activity conducted in the form of description link. Assignment copying will be difficult. Remedy: If any student doesn’t have the online data at home or busy with study hour then any other suitable activity may be allotted in genuine cases after receiving written request from the student/parent. 2) Active leaning method (2 to 4) Apart from mandatory ALM-1 more minimum 3 activities are to be conducted. But it is suggested to conduct more activities. Picture game: Equation game: Documentary making Group discussion A list of such activities is available in the appendix-1. Many more activities with procedure are also available in teacher’s manual. List given in appendix is for reference, Faculty is expected to create any new methods suitable according to their subject demand and students’ quality based on their experience. Steps/Procedure: Faculties shall conduct the activities involving students as given in the sample activities of appendix-2. Faculties shall record the proofs in the form of Google-tag photographs. (CRs can be asked to do this task if the faculty is not gadget expert) Faculties shall attach all the proofs and steps of activities conducted in the format given in the appendix-2 A format for keeping the track of activities will be kept with CRs in the class files

 Institute Mission

 To achieve excellence in education, research and consultancy by creating conducive environment that facilitate continuous improvement, with professionalism at its core and global outlook.
